q:
You went back to your home town after many years. It wasn't the same. (It / change / a lot)
It ___.

Somebody sang a song you didn't know. (I / not / hear / it / before)
I ___.

I invited Rachel to the party, but she couldn't come. (She / arrange / to do something else)
She ___.
a: She had arranged to do something else.

q:
You got to the cinema late last night. (The film / already / start)
The film ___.

Last year we went to Mexico for the first time. (We / not / be / there / before)
We ___.

I met Daniel last week after a long time. (I / not / see / him / for five years)
I ___.
a: I hadn't seen him for five years.

q:
I offered my friends food, but they weren't hungry. (They / just / have / lunch)
They ___.

Sam played his first game of tennis yesterday. (He / never / play / before)
He ___.

Laura went out this morning. I rang her doorbell, but there was no answer. (She / go / out)
She ___.

Joe came back from holiday a few days ago. I met him the same day. (He / just / come / back / from holiday)
He ___.
a: He had just come back from holiday.

q:
James sent Amy many emails, but she never replied. Yesterday he got a phone call from her. (He / be / surprised)
He ___.

I felt very tired when I got home. (I / go / straight to bed)
I ___.

The house was very quiet when I got home. (Everybody / go / to bed)
Everybody ___.

Mark travels a lot. When I first met him, (he / already / travel / round the world)
he ___.
a: he had already travelled round the world.

q:
Sorry I'm late. (The car / break / down / on my way here)
The car ___.
a: The car broke down on my way here.

q:
We were driving along the road when (we / see / a car / which / break / down)
we ___.
a: we saw a car which had broken down.
